MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Supp Discovery (Def. Royland Holman)
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan L. Kraft, Esq., Haapla, Thompson & Abern, LLP, Telephone 510-763-2324, appearing for Defendant Royland Holman;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROYLAND HOLMAN'S Motion To Compel Supp Discovery (Def. Royland Holman)

Granted in part and denied in part. Court considered both the late opposition and the late reply, in addition to the moving papers. Motion to compel denied as moot because Plaintiff served verified responses to Defendant's supplemental form interrogatories (set one) on January 28, 2019, prior to the filing of the instant motion on January 29, 2019. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $360. Plaintiff states that in a January 26, 2019 email, Defendant granted an extension to serve the discovery responses to January 28, 2019, and that Plaintiff complied with that deadline. The referenced January 26 email between counsel, which Plaintiff does not provide with her opposition, is attached as Exhibit G to the Declaration of Ryan Kraft. It states in relevant part: "I [Defendant] will be filing a motion to compel on Tuesday [January 29, 2019]. I will be requesting sanctions. If you deliver verified responses after I file the motion, I'm still going to ask for sanctions for having to file the motion. You may serve verified responses to our office on Monday [January 28, 2019] if you wish to avoid the motion." To avoid the instant motion, Plaintiff should have acted in a manner that was calculated to ensure that Defendant received the discovery responses on January 28 or at least received notice by that date that the responses were being served, so as to avoid filing the instant motion on January 29. Plaintiff does not offer any explanation as to why she served the discovery responses by mail on January 28, and did not alert Defendant that she was doing so. Plaintiff also does not provide any explanation as to why the discovery response were over four months late in the first place. Under these circumstances, the Court finds that some sanctions are warranted.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
